Order Status Tracker (Mark Project 2).action: Pulls live inventory status for every part on the weekly backorder report — including on-hand quantities, on-order records, and in-transit shipments.
Order Status Tracker (Mark Project 2).impact: Real-time inventory context is attached to every backordered line automatically, eliminating the need for a dozen people to independently look up the same parts.

Order Status Tracker (Mark Project 2).action: Enriches each backorder line with the live Dream data, flags parts that have no on-hand, on-order, or in-transit source as dead-end items with no resolution path.
Order Status Tracker (Mark Project 2).impact: The team instantly knows which backorders have a resolution path and which are truly stuck — turning a raw list into a prioritized action document.

Order Status Tracker (Mark Project 2).action: Generates and sends draft customer communications for affected backorder lines, pre-written with the relevant part status and estimated resolution — ready for review or direct send.
Order Status Tracker (Mark Project 2).impact: Customer updates that previously required individual lookups and manual writing across multiple team members are drafted in bulk automatically, saving hours of repetitive communication work each week.

Two Piece Rotor Catalog Builder.action: Scrapes the online catalogs of Gyro Disc, Alcon, and Brembo to extract all listed two-piece rotor fitments by make, model, and year.
Two Piece Rotor Catalog Builder.impact: A comprehensive competitive fitment dataset is built automatically across three catalogs — work that would take days to compile manually from separate websites.

Two Piece Rotor Catalog Builder.action: Cross-references the scraped fitment data against DFC's existing two-piece rotor parts to identify which vehicles DFC already covers and which represent potential inventory gaps.
Two Piece Rotor Catalog Builder.impact: Gaps in DFC's lineup are identified instantly against the competitive landscape, giving Dino a clear map of where new product development or sourcing opportunities exist.

Two Piece Rotor Catalog Builder.action: Sends automated outreach emails to targeted customers to collect demand data — asking which two-piece rotor fitments they need most.
Two Piece Rotor Catalog Builder.impact: Demand signals are gathered from real customers at scale, validating which gaps are worth filling before committing to inventory investment.

Two Piece Rotor Catalog Builder.action: Compiles all scraped fitments, DFC cross-reference results, gap flags, and customer demand responses into a single formatted reference sheet delivered to Dino.
Two Piece Rotor Catalog Builder.impact: Dino receives one clean, decision-ready document that consolidates weeks of research into a format he can act on immediately for product and inventory planning.

Meta Investigation.action: Scans all active Meta and TikTok ad copy — including headlines, primary text, and descriptions — and extracts the full text of every live ad for review.
Meta Investigation.impact: Every active ad is checked in one automated pass, eliminating the need to manually open and read individual ads across platforms to find specific language.

Meta Investigation.action: Reads through all extracted ad copy and flags any instance where a specified phrase or term appears, noting the exact field and verbatim copy where each match was found.
Meta Investigation.impact: Phrase-level compliance or quality issues are caught instantly across potentially hundreds of ads — a review that would take significant manual time is reduced to seconds.

Meta Investigation.action: Emails a structured report listing every flagged ad with the platform, campaign name, field name, and exact copy where the target phrase appeared.
Meta Investigation.impact: The requester receives a precise, actionable list they can act on immediately — no platform login or manual search required to locate the offending copy.

Paid Channel Chat bot.action: Pulls live ad performance data, copy, audience targeting, and spend metrics from all active Meta and TikTok campaigns into the agent's context.
Paid Channel Chat bot.impact: The chatbot has a complete, current picture of paid channel activity, enabling it to answer questions about performance, copy, or spend without anyone pulling a report manually.

Paid Channel Chat bot.action: Responds to on-demand questions about the paid channel data — surfacing performance insights, summarizing copy themes, comparing campaigns, or flagging anomalies based on the user's query.
Paid Channel Chat bot.impact: Anyone on the team can get instant, accurate answers about paid channel performance without needing platform access or analyst support — compressing research from hours to seconds.

Resume Bot.action: Monitors the jobs@r1concepts.com inbox and ingests each incoming application email, extracting the candidate's message and any attached resume file.
Resume Bot.impact: Every application is captured and routed automatically the moment it arrives, ensuring no candidate falls through the cracks due to inbox volume or manual triage delays.

Resume Bot.action: Saves each resume PDF into an organized Drive folder structure, making files accessible and searchable for the hiring team independent of the email inbox.
Resume Bot.impact: Resumes are stored in a permanent, organized location from day one — eliminating scattered email attachments and making candidate files easy to retrieve at any stage of hiring.

Resume Bot.action: Scores each resume against the qualifications of open roles, classifies the candidate as approved, denied, or paused (for roles currently on hold), and drafts the appropriate auto-reply message.
Resume Bot.impact: Every application receives a consistent, role-relevant evaluation in seconds — replacing hours of manual resume screening while ensuring no candidate waits days for a response.

Resume Bot.action: Logs every candidate decision — score, classification, role matched, and reply sent — into a master dashboard, with paused-role candidates automatically routed to a dedicated Pending tab.
Resume Bot.impact: The hiring team has a real-time, centralized view of every applicant and decision, making pipeline reviews instant and ensuring paused candidates are easy to revisit when roles reopen.

summary: The pipeline is running strong with 56 total agents across the board — 29 completed, 10 in progress, and 17 planned. No new agents were added this week, and no agents changed stages, so the pipeline remains steady from last week. The team continues to make solid progress working through the 10 active builds, with 17 planned agents still queued up and ready to move forward. Great work keeping the momentum going — more updates to come as things advance!
